### Direction
- [ ] I am arguing the **no-go** (the carrier cannot exist; the net-naturality theorem holds).
- [ ] I am exhibiting a **generative carrier** (a counterexample that flips the verdict).

### What the argument touches
- Which of the **five routes** / which lemma (N1, N2, the commutant-to-generated gap, the iter-7 η₀ baseline)?
- Where, precisely, does your construction avoid installing the signature / causal order (`n₁`) or a metric/Dirac/Krein datum (`η`) **by hand**? (This is the decisive step — every prior attempt smuggles one of these.)

### The argument
<!-- State it precisely, with the mathematics. -->

### Anti-crank checklist (required)
- [ ] Every nontrivial claim carries exactly one epistemic tag: `[ESTABLISHED]` / `[INFERENCE]` / `[SPECULATIVE]` / `[OPEN]` / `[CONTESTED]`.
- [ ] No fabricated citation, number, theorem, or arXiv ID — every reference web-verified against the live source (a missing reference is fine; a fake one is disqualifying).
- [ ] Red-teamed against the relevant no-go theorems (Weinberg–Witten, Haag, Reeh–Schlieder, …) **and** against this wiki's own closures (the dS cardinality no-go; HYP-CKV-VACUITY; the iter-12 Krein closure).
- [ ] It does not "explain everything and predict nothing."
